source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
title: Ensure usage of the 'root' account is monitored

input_row.source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure usage of the 'root' account is monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for 'root' login attempts to detect unauthorized use or attempts to use the root account.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring 'root' account logins will provide visibility into the use of a fully privileged account and the opportunity to reduce its usage.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> o Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> o Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group- name> 3. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: "filterPattern": "{ $.userIdentity.type = "Root" && $.userIdentity.invokedBy NOT EXISTS && $.eventType != "AwsServiceEvent" }" 4. Note the <root-usage-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 5. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <root-usage-metric> captured in step 4: aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<root-usage-metric>]' 6. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 7. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for 'root' account usage and uses the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name `<trail-log-group-name>` -- filter-name `<root-usage-metric>` --metric-transformations metricName= `<root-usage-metric>` ,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 -- filter-pattern '{ $.userIdentity.type = "Root" && $.userIdentity.invokedBy NOT EXISTS && $.eventType != "AwsServiceEvent" }' Note : You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 2. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note : You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note : Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 3. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note : You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 4. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name `<root-usage-alarm>` -- metric-name `<root-usage-metric>` --statistic Sum --period 300 -- threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold -- evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns_topic_arn> References: 1. CCE-79188-9 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/receive-cloudtrail- log-files-from-multiple-regions.html 3. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudwatch-alarms- for-cloudtrail.html 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/SubscribeTopic.html Additional Information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored
input_row.references: 1. CCE-79188-9 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/receive-cloudtrail- log-files-from-multiple-regions.html 3. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudwatch-alarms- for-cloudtrail.html 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/SubscribeTopic.html Additional Information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control can be fully verified using AWS APIs and CLI, as it involves checking the configuration of AWS services such as CloudTrail, CloudWatch, and SNS, all of which are accessible and verifiable via AWS APIs.

- ✓ Can AWS APIs query this property? → Yes, AWS APIs can query CloudTrail trail status, CloudWatch Logs, and SNS subscriptions.
- ✓ Is this a standard AWS service property (encryption, logging, access control, network rules)? → Yes, it's related to logging and monitoring configurations in standard AWS services.
- ✓ Does boto3 SDK have methods to check this? → Yes, Boto3 has methods for CloudTrail and CloudWatch.
- ✓ Does AWS CLI have commands to retrieve this? → Yes, AWS CLI commands exist for all requirements detailed in the audit steps.
- ✓ Is it about IAM, S3, EC2, VPC, CloudTrail, Config, KMS, etc.? → Yes, it involves CloudTrail and CloudWatch settings.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_root_usage_monitoring_setup

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program would perform the following checks:

1. **Verify Multi-Region CloudTrail is Active:**
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()` to list cloud trails.
   - Check for trails with `"IsMultiRegionTrail": True` and `"IsLogging": True` using `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_trail_status()`.

2. **Ensure Management Events are Captured:**
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_event_selectors()` to verify `IncludeManagementEvents` is set to `True` and `ReadWriteType` is set to `All`.

3. **Ensure Metric Filter for Root Account Usage Exists:**
   - Use `boto3.client('logs').describe_metric_filters(logGroupName=<trail-log-group-name>)` to check filters with the pattern `{ $.userIdentity.type = "Root" && $.userIdentity.invokedBy NOT EXISTS && $.eventType != "AwsServiceEvent" }`.

4. **Verify CloudWatch Alarm for Root Usage:**
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()` to ensure a metric alarm exists for the metric corresponding to root login attempts.

5. **Ensure SNS Topic and Subscription Exists:**
   - Validate that the alarm is configured to trigger an SNS topic using `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()`.
   - Use `boto3.client('sns').list_subscriptions_by_topic()` to ensure there is at least one subscriber to the SNS topic.

- **Pass Criteria:** All steps confirm existence and correct configuration of resources and settings.
- **Fail Criteria:** Any missing configuration or incorrect setting as per the steps above results in a fail.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:** The classification as Automated is correct. The control involves verifying configurations and settings that can be fully checked using AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI.
- **AWS APIs/boto3/CLI Capabilities:** The assessment correctly identifies that AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI can be used to query and verify the necessary configurations for CloudTrail, CloudWatch, and SNS.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 9

- **Automation Details:** The automation steps provided are technically accurate. The use of boto3 methods like `describe_trails()`, `get_trail_status()`, `get_event_selectors()`, `describe_metric_filters()`, `describe_alarms()`, and `list_subscriptions_by_topic()` are appropriate for verifying the configurations.
- **Program Name:** The program name `aws_cloudtrail_root_usage_monitoring_setup` is descriptive and clear, indicating the focus on monitoring root account usage via CloudTrail. However, it could be more specific about the security check aspect.
- **Completeness:** The steps cover all necessary checks for ensuring the root account usage is monitored effectively.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

1. **Program Name Suggestion:** Consider renaming to `aws_cloudtrail_root_account_monitoring_configuration` to better reflect the specific focus on configuration verification.
2. **Additional Considerations:** 
   - Ensure that the CloudTrail logs are being delivered to the specified CloudWatch Logs group by checking the `logGroupArn` in the trail configuration.
   - Verify the SNS topic's policy to ensure it allows CloudWatch to publish messages.
3. **Alternative Methods:** Consider using AWS Config rules to continuously monitor these configurations as an additional layer of compliance assurance.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is thorough and technically sound, with a clear understanding of AWS services and their configurations.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Keep as-is

- The audit approach and technical details are accurate and comprehensive. The minor improvements suggested do not necessitate a reclassification or major modification. The automated approach is well-justified and effectively utilizes AWS capabilities.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**
Both AI opinions correctly identify that all required checks for monitoring root account usage—such as verifying multi-region CloudTrail trails, CloudWatch metric filters, and alarms—are fully accessible via AWS APIs (boto3, AWS CLI). These configurations are standard AWS service properties and can be programmatically validated without manual intervention. There are no aspects of this control that require console-only or manual inspection.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
aws_cloudtrail_cloudwatch_root_account_usage_monitoring

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- AWS Resource/Service: CloudTrail Trails, CloudWatch Log Groups, CloudWatch Metric Filters, CloudWatch Alarms, SNS Topics
- API/CLI Method:
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()`
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_trail_status()`
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_event_selectors()`
  - `boto3.client('logs').describe_metric_filters()`
  - `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()`
  - `boto3.client('sns').list_subscriptions_by_topic()`
- Properties to Check:
  - At least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail (`IsMultiRegionTrail=True`, `IsLogging=True`)
  - Trail is logging management events (`IncludeManagementEvents=True`)
  - CloudTrail is delivering logs to a CloudWatch Log Group
  - CloudWatch Log Group has a metric filter for root account usage (filter pattern for root activity)
  - CloudWatch Alarm is configured on this metric filter and is enabled
  - Alarm notifies an SNS topic with at least one valid subscription
- Pass Criteria:
  - All above properties are present and correctly configured
- Fail Criteria:
  - Any of the above properties are missing, misconfigured, or not enabled

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**
Both AI opinions agreed on the automated approach. The only minor difference was in the suggested program name; I selected a name that is both descriptive and specific to the control’s intent, following the required naming convention.
